branching_policy.continuation_rule: Continue development in v14 as long as new capabilities can be added additively without breaking or distorting the current architecture.
branching_policy.v15_definition: v15 is not a version increment but a structural shift where the organizing center of the system changes (e.g., identity core, agent spawning, orchestration layers).
switch_conditions: The next required capability cannot be integrated into v14 without architectural distortion
A new layer changes the organizing center of the system (e.g., identity-first instead of field-first)
Maintaining the feature inside v14 would create false cohesion or hidden coupling
A parallel v15 path demonstrates equal or better behavior on agreed baseline checks

execution_policy.v14_role: stable executable reference and active development line
execution_policy.v15_role: emergent architecture, explored but not primary until switch conditions are met
execution_policy.requirement: All new work must preserve v14 behavior unless explicitly behind a flag or side path

branching_policy.design_principle: v15 is adopted when it becomes necessary, not when it becomes visible.
